_note: Category labels 'The Chain', 'Null Signal', and 'Fracture' are kept as separate stat-block groupings for table convenience, but per CAMPAIGN/villains.json they are three operational divisions of a single syndicate run by Undertow. Rank-and-file enemies in each category genuinely don't know about the other two divisions — don't let captured grunts leak that connection; only Undertow-tier NPCs know the whole shape.

enemies.name: Combat Drone Mk.I
enemies.description: Standard exam drone. Bipedal chassis, rubber-coated shock emitters. Non-lethal by design but hits hard enough to matter. Moves in arcs, fires on nearest student.

attacks.name: Shock Blast
attacks.notes: Non-lethal

traits: Non-lethal: all damage dealt reduces HP but targets don't die — they're knocked out at 0
Constructed: immune to charm, fear, and poison
Exam Point: destroying one grants 1 point toward exam score
enemies.tactics: Moves toward nearest student and fires. No target priority logic. Will not pursue fleeing students — just acquires new target. Trivially easy to trick.

enemies.name: Combat Drone Mk.II
enemies.description: Heavier exam drone. Armoured plating, dual shock emitters, and a missile pod that makes students reconsider their positioning. Worth three times the points.

attacks.name: Dual Shock Blast
attacks.notes: Non-lethal

attacks.name: Missile Burst
attacks.notes: DC 13 DEX save, half on success. Recharge 5-6. Non-lethal.

traits: Non-lethal: all damage is non-lethal
Constructed: immune to charm, fear, and poison
Armoured: resistant to piercing damage
Exam Point: destroying one grants 3 points
enemies.tactics: Prioritises students who destroyed Mk.I drones (most threatening student by score). Uses Missile Burst when 2+ students are within 10ft of each other. Retreats 10ft before firing if adjacent.

enemies.name: Sentinel Boss Drone
enemies.description: The finale of the entrance exam. A large, heavily armoured command drone that drops into the arena when Oshiro judges the class is ready to be tested as a unit. Designed to be unable to solo — the class must cooperate to bring it down.

attacks.name: Railgun Burst
attacks.notes: Non-lethal. Targets 1 creature.

attacks.name: Stomp
attacks.notes: DC 14 STR save or knocked prone. Non-lethal.

attacks.name: Suppression Field
attacks.notes: Area becomes difficult terrain while active. Students must use double movement to close distance. Toggle on/off each turn.

traits: Non-lethal: all damage is non-lethal
Constructed: immune to charm, fear, and poison
Phase Armour: resistant to all damage until its HP drops below 40, then loses this trait
Exam Point: destroying it grants 10 points, split among students who dealt the final 20 HP
Designed to Force Cooperation: its Suppression Field punishes solo attackers — it takes focused burst damage from multiple directions to break through efficiently
enemies.tactics: Activates Suppression Field immediately. Targets the highest-scoring student with Railgun Burst. Uses Stomp when students cluster. The intent is not to 'win' but to hold out long enough that students must coordinate. Drops Suppression Field when below 20 HP — its last attempt to lure students in for a final Stomp.

enemies.name: Chain Grunt
enemies.description: Low-level Chain muscle. No quirk. Hired for physical work — moving things, intimidating people, holding doors. Knows enough not to ask questions and not enough to be a real threat to a student.

attacks.name: Grab
attacks.notes: DC 12 STR to escape. While grappled, target is restrained and can't move.

traits: Pack Fighter: deals +1d4 damage when adjacent ally is also in melee
Surrender Threshold: at 0 HP, doesn't die — immediately surrenders and spills nothing useful ('I just do pickup and delivery, I swear')
enemies.tactics: Tries to grapple the smallest-looking student so an ally can beat on them. Not courageous — will flee at 5HP if no Chain enforcer is present to stop them.

enemies.name: Chain Enforcer
enemies.description: Mid-tier Chain operative. Has a minor quirk and uses it. Knows the organisation's first-layer structure — cells, not leadership. Disciplined enough not to run, smart enough to know when a fight is lost.

attacks.name: Shockwave Clap (Quirk)
attacks.notes: DC 13 STR save or pushed 10 ft. Recharge 5-6.

traits: Hardened: resistant to bludgeoning damage
Command Presence: Chain Grunts within 30 ft can't be frightened and deal +1d4 damage
Composure: advantage on saves against fear and charm
enemies.tactics: Gets into melee and holds position. Uses Shockwave Clap when 2+ students are adjacent. Will not abandon wounded allies — will drag them away before retreating. If captured: gives false name, asks for a lawyer, says nothing about the Chain beyond 'I'm a freelancer.'

enemies.name: Chain Hardskin
enemies.description: A Chain quirk-user with hardening ability. Dense, slow, nearly impossible to hurt with normal attacks. Has a Chain tattoo partially visible under a collar if someone looks. Doesn't know who Undertow is — just knows who pays them.

attacks.name: Hardened Rush
attacks.range: Charge up to 20 ft then strike
attacks.notes: Target makes DC 14 STR save or knocked prone. Can only be used if Hardskin moved at least 10 ft.

traits: Skin of Steel: resistant to bludgeoning, slashing, and piercing damage from non-magical sources
Quirk Weakness: vulnerable to lightning damage (double damage)
Chain Mark: a partial tattoo is visible under the collar. DC 15 Perception to spot, DC 12 Investigation to identify as an organisation mark.
Immovable: immune to the prone condition while Hardskin stance is active (toggle off as bonus action)
enemies.tactics: Absorbs hits without flinching, trying to demoralise students. Aims for Hardened Rush to knock someone prone, then stands over them. Doesn't talk during a fight. If students discover the lightning weakness, their satisfaction is visible even through the stoicism.

enemies.name: Chain Blaster
enemies.description: Long-range Chain operative. Energy projection quirk. Prefers distance and cover. Hired for jobs that need firepower without getting close. Nervous when cornered.

attacks.name: Multi-Bolt
attacks.notes: Fires 3 bolts, each must target a different creature. Bonus action.

traits: Overcharge: when at 10 HP or below, attacks deal +1d6 damage but Blaster takes 1d4 recoil damage each time they attack
Cover Seeker: while behind half or full cover, attack rolls against Blaster are made at disadvantage
enemies.tactics: Stays at maximum range. Uses Multi-Bolt to spread pressure across multiple students. Fires from behind cover if possible. At 10 HP, enters Overcharge — becomes more dangerous but is now hurting themselves. Will try to run if cover is removed or students close to melee.

enemies.name: Hired Muscle
enemies.description: No quirk, no organisation affiliation, just size and experience. Hired for intimidation or physical enforcement. Treats this like a professional job — no more violence than contracted, no personal grudges.

attacks.name: Haymaker
attacks.notes: Once per combat. On hit, target is stunned until end of their next turn (DC 14 CON save to negate stun).

traits: Shrug It Off: once per short rest, reduce incoming damage by 1d6+2 as a reaction
Professional: will not attack fallen, unconscious opponents unless instructed. Not a monster — just employed.
enemies.tactics: Targets whoever looks most dangerous. Uses Haymaker immediately against the most mobile student. If the job is clearly lost, stops fighting and says 'I'm done.' — he considers a fight professionally over when the odds are obvious. Will cooperate with authorities if his employer is dead or gone.

enemies.name: Street Villain
enemies.description: An unlicensed quirk user in over their head. Desperate, not dangerous. Usually committing crimes out of necessity rather than ideology. High role-play potential. Low threat. The kind of encounter that teaches students about the grey zones.
traits: Minor Quirk (DM chooses): weak energy burst (1d4 fire, 20ft), minor hardening (+1 AC, 1 minute), brief invisibility (1 round, recharge on 5-6), minor levitation (hover 5ft, move 20ft, no weight-bearing)
Panicked: disadvantage on attacks if reduced below half HP
Wants Out: will attempt to flee if an escape route opens. If cornered, surrenders. Will talk if treated well — might mention neighbourhood changes, the Chain's name if they've heard it.
enemies.tactics: Erratic. Uses quirk first, panics when it doesn't work, tries to run. Best encounter resolution is often social rather than combat. Has information if students choose de-escalation.

enemies.name: Speedster Villain
enemies.description: Fast, aggressive, overconfident. Thinks speed is an automatic win. Hasn't fought Ren. Will learn something today.

attacks.name: Rapid Strike
attacks.notes: Can attack twice (Extra Attack).

attacks.name: Blitz
attacks.range: Move up to full speed in straight line then strike
attacks.notes: Target makes DC 14 DEX save or knocked prone. Can be used once per turn as part of movement.

traits: Evasion: when subjected to a DEX save for half damage, takes no damage on success and half on failure
Fleet-Footed: opportunity attacks against this creature have disadvantage
Overconfident: if this villain goes 2 rounds without being hit, they become reckless (attacks at advantage but attack rolls against them also have advantage)
enemies.tactics: Opens with Blitz to establish dominance. Rapid Strikes from multiple angles. Never stays in one place. Gets cocky if no one can land hits. Actually vulnerable to anything that locks movement — grapple, restraint, or Ren just being faster.

enemies.name: Bruiser Villain
enemies.description: A heavyweight quirk-user — growth and density enhancement. Hits like a structure. Thinks like a sledgehammer. End-of-Tier-1 or early-Tier-2 threat. Not appropriate as a first encounter.

attacks.name: Ground Pound
attacks.notes: DC 15 STR save or knocked prone. Recharge 5-6.

traits: Titan Form (Quirk): as a bonus action, grows to 10ft. Gains +2 AC (total 17), attacks deal +1d6, reach becomes 10ft. Reverts if knocked unconscious.
Resilient: at the start of each turn, the first 3 damage from any source is ignored (not resistance — flat reduction)
Slow Burn: takes 2 full rounds to warm into Titan Form. Uses it immediately on round 1 — be ready by round 3.
enemies.tactics: Activates Titan Form immediately. Focuses one target to maximum effect — doesn't split attention. Uses Ground Pound when students cluster trying to flank. The fight against this enemy is about positioning, burst damage windows, and not letting anyone get isolated.

enemies.name: Trickster Villain
enemies.description: Illusion quirk. Annoying, evasive, difficult to pin down. Creates confusion rather than damage. The kind of villain who makes students feel like they're losing until someone figures out the trick.

attacks.name: Illusion Burst
attacks.notes: DC 13 INT save or blinded until end of next turn. Bonus action.

traits: Mirror Image: creates 3 illusory duplicates at the start of combat. When attacked, roll 1d4 — on a 1, the real Trickster is hit. On 2-4, a duplicate is struck and destroyed. Recharge 5-6.
Displacement: while Mirror Image is active, can use a reaction to move 15 ft without provoking opportunity attacks
enemies.tactics: Creates Mirror Image immediately. Uses Illusion Burst on the most dangerous student. Stays mobile, never adjacent if possible. The fight has a clear solution — dealing with duplicates, identifying the real target — and the satisfaction is in finding it.

enemies.name: Training Arena Bot
enemies.description: Heavy training robot used in formal combat drills. Armoured, slow, and telegraphs its attacks — designed to teach students positioning and timing rather than to win. Used in Capture the Flag and Combat Training arcs as demonstration or obstacle.

attacks.name: Heavy Piston
attacks.notes: Non-lethal. DM announces the target one round before the attack — the bot telegraphs its strikes.

attacks.name: Suppression Burst
attacks.notes: DC 13 STR save or pushed 10 ft. Non-lethal. Recharge 5-6.

traits: Armoured Shell: resistant to bludgeoning and piercing damage
Slow: cannot take reactions or opportunity attacks
Non-lethal: all damage is non-lethal
Telegraphed: at the end of the bot's turn, DM announces which target it will attack next round. This is the training mechanic — students should be reacting to that information.
enemies.tactics: Slow advance. Announces target one round ahead. Uses Suppression Burst when students stop moving. Not designed to win — designed to teach.

enemies.name: Null Signal Analyst
enemies.description: Low-level field operative. Observes, documents, and backs up Specialists. Usually armed with a suppression baton and a data capture device. They are paid to not ask questions.

attacks.name: Suppression Baton
attacks.notes: On hit, target makes DC 12 CON save or their quirk use costs an additional action until end of next turn.

attacks.name: Tranq Dart
attacks.damage: 1d4 piercing + DC 12 CON save or Poisoned for 1 minute
attacks.notes: Single use per encounter.

traits: Cell Discipline: Will not retreat unless half the cell is down. Breaks routing saves at advantage.
Data Collar: Carries a device that transmits biometric data on any quirk user they touch to Null Signal. Destroying it (AC 10, 5 HP) stops the feed.
enemies.tactics: Hangs back at 20-30 ft. Uses Tranq Dart early to create a poisoned target for the Specialist to focus. Never engages in melee unless forced.

enemies.name: Null Signal Specialist
enemies.description: Mid-rank operative with a suppression quirk or suppression tech equivalent. Acquisition's primary field unit. Trained in restraint and extraction — these people have done this before.

attacks.name: Restraint Cuffs
attacks.notes: On successful grapple, target is also Restrained. DC 14 STR to break. Cuffs have 20 HP and resistance to all damage.

attacks.name: Suppression Pulse (Tech)
attacks.notes: All creatures in cone make DC 13 CON save or their quirk is suppressed for 1 round. Recharge 5-6.

traits: Extraction Priority: If a target is Restrained and below half HP, Specialist attempts extraction (removing target from combat) instead of attacking. Requires one full action and no other threats adjacent.
Coordinated Takedown: When attacking a target already grappled by an ally, gains advantage on the attack roll.
enemies.tactics: Moves to grapple the most quirk-dependent PC. Calls Analysts to screen. Uses Suppression Pulse to create a window, then immediately closes to grapple. Works in pairs when possible.

enemies.name: Null Signal Operative
enemies.description: Elite field agent. Each Operative has a notable combat quirk and has been briefed on the target's abilities. They are not mercenaries — they are invested in the outcome.

attacks.name: Quirk Strike
attacks.notes: The DM should choose a specific quirk for each Operative — treat as a flavoured version of the damage type.

attacks.name: Counter Protocol
attacks.notes: When targeted by a quirk attack, Operative may make a DC 14 INT save. On success, halves incoming damage and identifies the quirk's weakness.

attacks.name: Multiattack
attacks.notes: Makes two Quirk Strike attacks per action.

traits: Briefed: Operative has full dossiers on Class 1-A PCs. On the first round, they correctly predict the PC's most likely action and are not surprised by it.
Field Professionalism: Immune to Frightened. Advantage on saves against Charmed.
Tactical Comm: As a bonus action, can relay target intel — all Null Signal allies gain advantage on their next attack against one target this turn.
enemies.tactics: Opens by identifying the most dangerous PC and calling it out to the cell. Uses Counter Protocol aggressively — these people have fought quirk users before. Protects Analysts from focus fire.

enemies.name: Splice (Ryo Karashi)
enemies.description: Head of Acquisition. Boss encounter. Prefers incapacitation to damage — he wants the class intact for study. Should be fought with his Operatives screening for him.

attacks.name: Modification Touch
attacks.notes: Target makes DC 16 CON save or suffers one of: Disadvantage on attacks, halved speed, or must drop held item. Gloves off: +1d8 necrotic.

attacks.name: Self-Reshape (Bonus)
attacks.notes: Extend reach by 5 ft OR gain +3 AC until start of next turn.

attacks.name: Acquisition Protocol
attacks.notes: Grappled target: DC 16 CON save or Paralysed until end of next turn. Splice can't move while using this.

attacks.name: Multiattack
attacks.notes: Two Modification Touch attacks.

traits: Glove Removal: Splice starts with gloves on. Removing them (bonus action) adds 1d8 necrotic to Modification Touch. He only does this when forced.
Briefed: Has dossiers on all PCs. On round 1, correctly anticipates the most dangerous PC's action.
Acquisition Priority: Below 40 HP, Splice stops attacking and attempts to withdraw with any incapacitated target he can carry.
enemies.tactics: Stays mobile, probes for grapple opportunities. Screened by Operatives. Targets support-role PCs or anyone already debuffed. Removes gloves at 50% HP. Attempts extraction if he has a paralysed target — he'd rather leave with one subject than fight to the end.

enemies.name: Fracture Cell Member
enemies.description: A true believer. Mostly young adults with minor or mid-range quirks who feel the system has failed them. They are not trained fighters. Some are scared. Most are angry. A few are genuinely dangerous. They wear a jagged-line symbol somewhere on their person.

attacks.name: Quirk Attack (Minor)
attacks.notes: Roll a d6 to determine damage type: 1-2 bludgeoning, 3-4 fire, 5 lightning, 6 slashing.

attacks.name: Improvised Weapon
attacks.notes: Used as fallback.

traits: Ideology Check: When a PC attempts to talk them down (CHA check DC 12), a Cell Member may hesitate — losing their action that turn but not fully standing down. DC 16 causes them to withdraw.
Not a Killer: Will not make attacks that could kill a downed target. Breaks from combat if forced into lethal choices.
Cell Loyalty: Has disadvantage on saves against being frightened by the class, but advantage if a Cell Leader is present.
enemies.tactics: Attacks in groups. Stays near Cell Leader if one is present. Easy to talk down if approached with genuine engagement rather than force. Will scatter if their leader is defeated.

enemies.name: Fracture Cell Leader
enemies.description: Experienced member with a solid combat quirk and genuine tactical sense. Leads a cell of 4-8 members. Protective of their people — this shapes their decisions more than ideology does.

attacks.name: Multiattack
attacks.notes: Two Quirk Strikes.

traits: Rally: As a bonus action, Cell Leader grants all Fracture Cell Members within 30 ft advantage on their next attack roll or saving throw.
Protect the People: If a Cell Member is downed, Cell Leader immediately targets whoever downed them, gaining advantage on attacks against that target for 1 round.

Ideological Argument: A PC who succeeds on a CHA (Persuasion) DC 15 check can force the Cell Leader to hesitate for 1 round. DC 20 ends the encounter without combat. This resets if the class is overtly aggressive.
Never Surrenders Alone: Will not surrender while any Cell Members are still up and able to retreat.
enemies.tactics: Positions behind Cell Members. Uses Rally at the start of combat. Targets the PC who's doing the most damage to their team. Willing to end the fight via argument if approached correctly — but only if the class hasn't already hurt someone they care about.

enemies.name: Voltage (Daisuke Ren)
enemies.description: Co-founder of Fracture. Boss encounter. Tactically intelligent — he reads the class and adapts. Use him to punish overconfidence. He fights to protect, not to win, which makes him unpredictable.

attacks.name: Shock Strike
attacks.notes: Target makes DC 16 CON save or movement halved until end of next turn.

attacks.name: Chain Discharge
attacks.notes: DC 16 DEX save. On fail: full damage + Stunned until end of next turn. Half on success.

attacks.name: Paralytic Surge
attacks.notes: Once per short rest. DC 16 CON save or Paralysed for 1 minute. Save repeats at end of each turn.

attacks.name: Multiattack
attacks.notes: Two Shock Strikes.

traits: Grounded: Immune to lightning damage. Advantage on saves against quirks affecting the nervous system.

Environmental Read: Voltage starts combat by identifying conductive surfaces (metal fences, wet ground, pipes). He has advantage on Chain Discharge attacks if he set up his position before combat started.
Adaptive: After the first round, Voltage correctly identifies the class's most powerful combo and positions to break it.
Protective Priority: If Shard or a Cell Leader is present and drops below 30 HP, Voltage immediately moves to their position and uses Paralytic Surge on whoever threatens them.
enemies.tactics: Picks his ground before combat if possible. Opens with Chain Discharge on clustered PCs. Targets fire or sonic quirk users who can arc through conductive surfaces. Knows Steel conducts electricity straight through it and baits hardened opponents into eating a hit they think is safe. Willing to take a hit to get Paralytic Surge off on the biggest threat.

enemies.name: Undertow (Soryu Hatch)
enemies.description: Founder and strategist of The Chain. Permeation quirk — can turn intangible, pass through matter, and phase individual body parts. Never raises his voice.

enemies.name: Flashpoint (Goda Miruki)
enemies.description: The Chain's enforcer. Cremation quirk — blue-white flames hot enough to soften steel, that cling and continue burning. Overwhelming and unpredictable.

enemies.name: Riddle (Sae Ichiro)
enemies.description: The Chain's information broker and manipulator. Brainwashing quirk — commands implanted through voice. Prefers never to be in the room when things go wrong.

enemies.name: Kuma
enemies.description: The Chain's heavy. Gigantification quirk — grows to enormous size, dramatically increasing strength and durability. No alias, no pretense. Just Kuma.

enemies.name: Null (Aoi Tenshin)
enemies.description: Head of Null Signal. Erasure quirk — cancels the quirks of any person they make eye contact with. Calm, methodical, and terrifying in a quiet room.

enemies.name: Oshiro Daikichi
enemies.description: Homeroom teacher and combat training lead. Steel quirk — hardens skin to industrial-grade metal. Absolute unit. Students learn quickly not to pull punches in his class.

enemies.name: Fujisaki Nene
enemies.description: Support technology and gear crafting teacher. Fiber Master quirk — generates and controls high-tensile threads. Can restrain, redirect, and construct with them.

enemies.name: Hamagawa Rei
enemies.description: Hero ethics and law teacher. Erasure quirk — nullifies quirks on eye contact. Keeps her goggles on during class. Students who think ethics is a soft subject reconsider after sparring with her.

enemies.name: Yuzuki Taro
enemies.description: Physical training and conditioning teacher. Muscle Augmentation quirk — dramatically enhances his own physical capability on demand. Has never needed to go above 40%.

enemies.name: Shimoda Iku
enemies.description: Field operations and rescue tactics teacher. Weather Manipulation quirk — localised weather control including wind, rain, lightning, and fog. Runs the most chaotic outdoor exercises.

enemies.name: Miscellaneous Hero
enemies.description: A licensed hero of average ability. Competent, professional, and effective within their power set. Useful as an ally, a challenge, or a comparison point for the class.

enemies.name: Miscellaneous Villain
enemies.description: An unlicensed quirk user operating outside the law. Dangerous but not exceptional. Average across the board — the benchmark for what a villain looks like before the Chain gets involved.

enemies.name: Shard (Maki Torune)
enemies.description: Co-founder of Fracture. Boss encounter. She doesn't want to fight. She will fight. Area control specialist — the encounter is as much about navigating her Glass Field as defeating her. She should have talking opportunities built in.

attacks.name: Glass Volley
attacks.notes: Can target 2 creatures within 5 ft of each other as one action.

attacks.name: Glass Field
attacks.notes: Difficult terrain, 1 minute. Action to place.

attacks.name: Barrier (Reaction)
attacks.notes: Glass wall: 10 ft wide, 10 ft tall, 30 HP. Must destroy barrier to hit her.

attacks.name: Glass Rain
attacks.notes: Recharge 5-6. DC 16 DEX save or take full damage + begin bleeding (1d4/turn, action to stop). Half on success, no bleed.

traits: Won't Kill: Shard does not make attacks that would reduce a target to 0 HP if she knows they're already below half health. She will incapacitate, not kill.

Debate Opening: Once per encounter, as an action, Shard stops fighting and addresses one PC directly. That PC makes a DC 14 WIS save. On fail, they are compelled to respond for 1 round (can't attack Shard, can still defend). On success, they are unaffected but Shard gains no further debate opportunities.
Glass Sight: Shard has tremorsense 30 ft via vibrations through her glass. Invisible creatures don't gain advantage against her within this range if they're standing on her glass.
enemies.tactics: Opens with Glass Field to control the space. Uses Barrier reactively to buy time for talking. Glass Volley at range. Uses Glass Rain when cornered. Always tries to create one conversation — if a PC engages honestly, she responds in kind, even mid-fight.
